original_abstract:
Abstract

**Background:** The Patient Perception of Arrhythmia Questionnaire (PPAQ) is a specialized tool developed to evaluate symptoms and health-related quality of life (QoL) in individuals with supraventricular tachycardia (SVT). Despite its clinical utility, a validated Polish version of this instrument has been lacking, restricting quality-of-life research within this specific patient population in Poland.

**Aim:** This study sought to translate the original English PPAQ into Polish and perform a cultural adaptation to establish its initial content validity.

**Methods:** The translation and adaptation process followed the International Society for Pharmacoeconomics and Outcomes Research (ISPOR) Principles of Good Practice. This structured approach included forward translation by independent linguists, reconciliation by clinical experts, back translation reviewed by the original author, and harmonisation. Finally, cognitive debriefing was conducted with a target population of 20 patients (12 males, mean age 54.9 ± 17.4 years) diagnosed with various types of SVT.

**Results:** The systematic translation process successfully produced a Polish version of the PPAQ. Subjective feedback during cognitive debriefing indicated that the questionnaire was user-friendly and accurately captured the patient experience. Key linguistic challenges identified during the process included the nuanced translation of specific symptom descriptors and the management of gender-specific grammatical structures inherent to the Polish language. These were resolved through consultation with electrophysiology specialists and by incorporating direct patient preferences during the debriefing phase.

**Conclusions:** The Polish version of the PPAQ demonstrated strong initial content validity and was well-received by patients. It serves as a reliable tool for assessing symptom burden and QoL in Polish-speaking SVT patients. Furthermore, the strategies employed to address linguistic and cultural barriers provide a framework for future adaptations of the PPAQ into other languages.
humanized_abstract:
Abstract

Background: The Patient Perception of Arrhythmia Questionnaire (PPAQ) was designed to examine the patient’s perception of the symptomatology of supraventricular tachycardia (SVT) and its influence on quality of life. Until now there has not been a validated version for Poland, which has made impossible an analysis of the quality of life in these patients in Poland.

**Title:** Polish version of Physical Activity Questionnaire (PPAQ) – initial validation after cultural adaptation.

**Methods** This study applied the ISPOR Principles of Good Practice for Valuation: Drug Diagnostics and other Medical Tests, to perform the translation and adaptation process. The method involved: a) a forward translation, executed by independent linguists, b) a reconciliation by an expert clinical group, c) a review and reconciliation of the back translation executed by the original author, and d) a harmonisation step. Finally, a cognitive debriefing exercise was applied to 20 patients (12 men, mean age of 54.9 ± 17.4 years, suffering from different types of Supra Ventricular Tachycardia).

**Results:** The translation was done systematically and the resulting Polish version of PPAQ was validated using a cognitive debriefing interview. The patients found the PPAQ user-friendly and it seemed to correspond to their experience with seizures. Problems that arose during translation concerned the definition of the particular symptoms (especially the exact wording referring to the sensation of electric current in the muscles), as well as gender difference presented in grammatical structures of the Polish language. These problems were solved after consulting an electrophysi Crime specialist, and due to the suggestions given by the patients during the cognitive debriefing interview.

**Conclusions:** The Polish version of PPAQ proved high initial content validity and was found satisfactory by the patients. It may be recommended for assessment of symptom burden and health related quality of life (HRQoL) in SVT disease in Polish language speaking population. The methods and procedures developed during translation process may be successfully applied to translate the PPAQ into other languages.

Abstract

**Background:** The Patient Perception of Arrhythmia Questionnaire (PPAQ) is a specialized tool developed to evaluate symptoms and health-related quality of life (QoL) in individuals with supraventricular tachycardia (SVT). Finally, cognitive debriefing was conducted with a target population of 20 patients (12 males, mean age 54.9 ± 17.4 years) diagnosed with various types of SVT.

**Results:** The systematic translation process successfully produced a Polish version of the PPAQ. Subjective feedback during cognitive debriefing indicated that the questionnaire was user-friendly and accurately captured the patient experience. Key linguistic challenges identified during the process included the nuanced translation of specific symptom descriptors and the management of gender-specific grammatical structures inherent to the Polish language. **Conclusions:** The Polish version of the PPAQ demonstrated strong initial content validity and was well-received by patients.
